title: quercus × neopalmeri
intro: Quercus × neopalmeri, also known as Palmer's oak or Neopalmer oak, is a hybrid plant of Quercus palmeri and Quercus oblongifolia, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been studied for its medicinal properties, including its flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × neopalmeri are thought to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Quercus × neopalmeri is sometimes used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use and preparation may vary depending on the specific application and desired benefits.
